## Part 1: Exhaustive Feature Architecture

### 1\. Form Builder Modalities & Creation Helper (The Setup)

### reForm provides three distinct ways for a creator/builder to design forms:

* ### **Manual/Standard Form Builder (Free/Basic Tier):** A drag-and-drop visual canvas (similar to Google Forms) where creators manually add, arrange, and configure static form blocks.

* ### **Text-Based AI Chatbot Helper (Standard/Mid Tier):** A text-only chat window interface where the builder communicates with the AI assistant exclusively through typing. The builder describes the form goals, and the chatbot generates the structure. No microphone or audio output is active in this mode.

* ### **Voice-Conversational AI Helper (Premium Tier):** A real-time, bi-directional voice chat environment. The builder speaks directly into their microphone, and the AI speaks questions and instructions out loud back to the builder (mirroring the form filler's voice interview experience).

  * ### **Unified Voice Components:** This mode reuse the conversational block and speech-to-text / text-to-speech engine, pre-configured specifically for the form-architect domain.

  * ### **Document-Driven Generation:** Across both AI modes, builders can upload files (PDFs, Word documents, Excel spreadsheets) alongside their text/voice instructions to auto-populate form structures (e.g. creating tests from curriculum files).

  * ### **Interactive Real-Time Preview (Split-Screen):** While chatting or speaking to either AI assistant, a live-updating split-screen preview of the form is displayed on the right, showing blocks constructing and arranging in real-time.

  * ### **Granular Block Selection & Comment-to-Update Loop:** The builder reviews the blocks generated by the chatbot and selects which blocks to keep. Additionally, by hovering over any block in the preview, the builder can type/speak inline suggestions or comments. Once comments are added, the builder clicks **Update** to trigger the AI to refine and rebuild the form structure to their exact liking.

### 2\. Form Elements & Question Types (The Building Blocks)

### reForm supports standard inputs (static or generated) and a powerful AI block:

* ### **Standard Inputs:** Short Text, Long Text/Paragraph, Email, Phone Number (with country code auto-detect), and URL.

* ### **Selection Inputs:** Multiple Choice (Radio), Checkboxes (Multi-select), Dropdowns, and Image-Choice (Clickable picture grids).

* ### **Quantitative Inputs:** Rating Scales (1-5 stars, 1-10 numbers), Opinion Sliders (e.g., "Strongly Disagree" to "Strongly Agree"), and Net Promoter Score (NPS).

* ### **Complex Inputs:** Date/Time Picker, Matrix/Grid (rating multiple rows on a shared scale), and Signature Pad.

* ### **File & Media Uploads:** Sleek drag-and-drop zones supporting Images (.png, .jpg), Documents (.pdf, .docx, .txt, .xlsx, .csv), and Audio/Video snippets.

* ### **The AI Conversational Block (The Brain):** A modular block that hands conversational control over to the Gemini AI Engine to conduct a fluid, dynamic interview based on prompt targets.

### 3\. The Omni-Modal AI Conversational Engine (Form Filler Experience)

### When a user encounters the AI Conversational Block, the interface transitions into a live, media-rich interview:

* ### **Tri-Modal Input Sync:** End-users can seamlessly switch between typing, speaking into their microphone, or clicking dynamically generated on-screen UI buttons.

* ### **Real-Time Speech-to-Text & Text-to-Speech:** As the AI speaks, its words type out in a chat-bubble UI. As the user speaks, their speech is transcribed and rendered on-screen in real-time so they see exactly what the system is capturing.

* ### **Goal-Oriented Conversations:** Builders define targeted information goals (e.g., *"Verify Java experience, ask about monolith vs microservices, and confirm salary expectations"*). The AI conversational engine guides the conversation, dynamically asking questions until all goals are answered.

* ### **AI Guardrails & Hallucination Moderation Filter:** A configurable safety filter preventing the conversational block from discussing topics outside the form’s objective. If a candidate asks the recruitment AI to write Python scripts, play a game, or discuss politics, the AI politely redirects them back to the interview goals.

* ### **Dynamic UI Generation:** The AI streams JSON schemas to the frontend, rendering clickable UI elements (e.g., choice buttons, sliders) dynamically inside the chat interface to save user typing/speaking effort.

* ### **Contextual File Prompts (Dynamic Dropzones):** The AI can request files contextually (e.g., *"Please upload a copy of your ID"*), dynamically materializing drag-and-drop fields in the chat flow.

* ### **Live Document/Vision Analysis (Gemini Vision):** Files uploaded mid-conversation are immediately processed by Gemini Vision, allowing the AI to discuss the file's content in subsequent questions.

* ### **Multi-Language Auto-Detect:** Instantly detects the user's language, responds audibly in that language, and translates all on-screen UI buttons.

* ### **Abuse Prevention & Session Safeguards:** To prevent credit wastage (e.g., a filler leaving a voice session active with background noise or silence), the system monitors idle status. It warns the user and automatically closes the session/saves the form if silence or inactivity exceeds a threshold.

### 4\. Authentication, Roles, & Workspace Management

* ### **Multi-Provider Auth:** JWT-based secure login via Email/Password, Google OAuth2, and Facebook OAuth2.

* ### **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC):** Roles include ADMIN (billing and global settings), CREATOR (building and managing forms), and VIEWER (reading submissions and analytics).

* ### **Workspace Silos:** Multi-tenant workspace partitioning (e.g., agencies managing multiple client workspaces). All forms, keys, submission data, and webhooks are strictly isolated per workspace.

### 5\. Billing, Credits & Enterprise Monetization

* ### **Stripe Subscription Tiers:** Free (Basic forms, manual builder), Pro (AI Voice features, AI Co-Builder assistant), and Enterprise (Headless API access, high volume).

* ### **Credit Ledger System:** AI interactions (tokens/audio minutes) consume credits. Real-time balance checks prevent overages and abuse.

* ### **Credit Consumption Dashboard:** Analytics showing workspace-level and form-level credit burn rates.

### 6\. Form Publishing & Distribution

* ### **Hosted Public URLs:** Instant hosted landing pages (reform.app/f/my-form).

* ### **Custom Domains (CNAME):** Pointing forms to custom subdomains (survey.company.com).

* ### **Embed Options:** Copy-paste Iframe codes, Inline JS, and exit-intent popup triggers.

* ### **Headless JSON API:** Allows Enterprise developers to fetch the form structure and raw conversational branching logic, and build their own custom user interface.

* ### **Developer Documentation Portal:** A dedicated, interactive developer portal containing API endpoint references, authentication guides, payloads, and code snippets in popular languages (JS/TS, Python, Curl).

### 7\. Analytics & Data Actionability

* ### **Raw Submissions Table:** A filterable and exportable grid containing all collected response columns.

* ### **Playback Transcript Viewer:** Replay the complete conversation text transcript and listen to the user's recorded audio responses directly in the dashboard.

* ### **AI Auto-Summarization:** One-click summarization condensing long conversation transcripts into key bullet points.

* ### **Sentiment & Thematic Tagging:** Automated AI classification of responses (e.g., "Urgent", "Satisfied", "Frustrated") to filter feedback.

* ### **Analytics Visualization Dashboard:**

  * ### Auto-generated pie charts and bar graphs for quantitative/selection inputs.

  * ### **Academic/Quiz Scoring Stats:** For test/quiz forms, the dashboard calculates scores, displays overall average scores, highest/lowest scores, and a grade distribution curve.

### 8\. Enterprise API & Integrations

* ### **Event-Driven Webhooks:** Real-time webhooks delivering JSON payloads upon submission completion.

* ### **Native Integrations:** Oauth connections to HubSpot, Salesforce, Slack, and Google Sheets.

* ### **API Key Management:** Creators can generate and revoke secret API keys to extract submissions data programmatically.

### ---

## Part 2: In-Depth Workflows

### Workflow A: The Form Builder (Your Client/Creator)

#### Phase 1: Setup & Conception

1. ### **Onboarding:** John logs in and accesses his "HR Recruitment" Workspace. He clicks **New Form**.

2. ### **Creation Mode Selection:** He is presented with three choices:

   * ### *Create Manually:* Standard drag-and-drop workspace.

   * ### *AI Chatbot (Text):* Text-only chatbot pane on the left, split-screen preview on the right.

   * ### *AI Conversational Guide (Voice):* Interactive voice chat assistant on the left (AI talks out loud, builder replies via voice), split-screen preview on the right. John chooses the voice mode.

3. ### **Requirements Ingestion:** John speaks into his microphone: *"I want to recruit backend engineers. I have a syllabus/description of our tech stack in this PDF. Please build a form that asks for contact info, resumes, and then asks technical interview questions."* He uploads the PDF.

4. ### **Real-Time Generation & Review:**

   * ### The voice chatbot speaks back: *"I've analyzed the PDF. Let's create blocks for candidate details, resume upload, and a technical interview stage. I've populated the preview pane with these blocks."*

   * ### Simultaneously, the interactive preview pane on the right dynamically populates with 10 form blocks.

5. ### **Refinement Loop:**

   * ### John hovers over the generated "File Dropzone for Resume" block and types a comment: *"Change file size limit to 10MB."*

   * ### He hovers over the "AI Conversational Interview" block and comments: *"Add a goal to ask about PostgreSQL indexing."*

   * ### He clicks **Update**. The chatbot re-runs, and the preview panel updates the form configuration in real-time. He selects which blocks to keep and proceeds.

#### Phase 2: Configuring the AI Brain

1. ### **AI Block Tuning:** John selects the generated "Conversational AI Engine" block.

2. ### **Setting the Persona & Prompts:** He writes: *"You are an experienced technical recruiter. Review their resume. Ask 3 technical questions based on Spring Boot, PostgreSQL, and monolith architecture. Keep it professional."*

3. ### **Setting Goals & Constraints:** John sets the target goals the AI must extract. He turns on "Enable Dynamic File Requests" (allowing the AI to ask for diagrams or code files) and sets an abuse prevention limit: *"Max 5 minutes or 6 conversation turns."*

4. ### **Model Selection:** John selects Gemini Pro for high-fidelity resume parsing.

#### Phase 3: Publishing & Analysis

1. ### **Distribution:** John publishes the form and embeds it on his careers page. He configures a webhook to push highly-qualified candidate data directly to a Slack channel.

2. ### **Reviewing Submissions:** As applicants complete the form, John logs into the dashboard:

   * ### He views the **Analytics Visualization Dashboard** showing aggregated test scores and candidate skills.

   * ### He opens a candidate record, reviews the summarized transcript, and plays the audio files of candidate responses.

### ---

### Workflow B: The Form Filler (The End-User / Customer)

#### Phase 1: The Static Entry

1. ### **Arrival & Form Input:** Sarah arrives at the careers page and fills out her name, email, and drops her resume PDF in the static file dropzone.

2. ### **Transition:** She clicks **Begin Interview**. The screen fades into a focused, modern chat interface.

#### Phase 2: The AI Takeover (The Omni-Modal Experience)

1. ### **Warm Greeting:** A pleasant voice speaks: *"Hi Sarah, thanks for applying. I see you have Spring Boot experience. What is the most challenging Spring project you have built?"*

2. ### **Synchronized Transcription:** As the voice speaks, the text renders on screen.

3. ### **Tri-Modal Response:** Sarah presses the microphone icon to speak. As she speaks, a real-time transcript shows her voice text dynamically rendering on screen.

4. ### **Inactivity monitoring:** If Sarah walks away or remains silent for more than 45 seconds, the AI says: *"Are you still there? Let me know if you need more time."* If silence continues, the session safely times out to prevent credit drainage.

#### Phase 3: Dynamic UI & Contextual Intelligence

1. ### **Dynamic UI Choice:** The AI responds: *"Spring Boot is excellent. Which database did you pair with it?"* Beneath the chat bubbles, three option buttons fade in: \[PostgreSQL\], \[MySQL\], \[MongoDB\].

2. ### **Simple Interaction:** Sarah clicks \[PostgreSQL\]. The click registers immediately, and the AI continues.

3. ### **Dynamic Dropzone:** The AI says: *"Great. Do you happen to have a diagram of your database schema?"* An inline dropzone titled *"Upload Database Diagram"* slides up.

4. ### **Vision Processing:** Sarah uploads a JPEG of her schema. Gemini Vision analyzes it: *"I see your indexing strategy here. Very clean setup. Let's move on."*

#### Phase 4: Completion & Submission

1. ### **Goal Completion:** Once the AI checks off all of John's goals (or hits the turn limit), it wraps up: *"Thank you, Sarah. We will review your answers by Friday."*

2. ### **Success State:** The chat fades to a beautifully animated success screen.

3. ### **Backend Dispatch:** The session closes. The transcript, audio recordings, files, and score evaluations are stored in the PostgreSQL database, and the Slack webhook fires.

### ---

## Part 3: Under-the-Hood Agentic AI Architecture

### reForm utilizes a multi-agent system powered by Gemini models to coordinate distinct aspects of form creation, conversation orchestration, safety, and document interpretation:

### ![][image1]

### 1\. Form Architect Agent (Co-Builder Assistant)

* ### **Role:** Translates requirements from the builder (entered via voice/text or extracted from uploaded reference documents) into structural form blueprints.

* ### **Responsibilities:**

  * ### Parses user descriptions to suggest optimal block layouts (e.g. static inputs vs conversational blocks).

  * ### Accepts block-level comments from the preview pane, updates target variables, and regenerates the form JSON.

* ### **Core Technology:** Gemini Flash (for low latency structure generation) mapping instructions directly to block schema schemas.

### 2\. Conversation Orchestrator Agent (Interviewer)

* ### **Role:** Manages the active state of the conversational block during the filler's submission experience.

* ### **Responsibilities:**

  * ### Extracts target information goals and dynamically evaluates if goals have been fulfilled.

  * ### Decides whether to stream dynamic UI choice buttons or materialise file dropzones based on conversation flow.

* ### **Core Technology:** Gemini Pro (for deep reasoning, memory context, and multi-turn conversation tracking).

### 3\. Guardrail & Moderation Agent (Safety Layer)

* ### **Role:** Performs inline sanitization and goal validation during multi-turn interviews.

* ### **Responsibilities:**

  * ### Intercepts off-topic queries (e.g., candidate asking the AI to code or write essays) and redirects them politely to the form targets.

  * ### Monitors dialog against system boundaries to ensure the AI doesn't hallucinate statements, pricing, or commitments on behalf of the form builder.

* ### **Core Technology:** A lightweight, localized classification agent running synchronously alongside the Conversation Orchestrator.

### 4\. Vision & Document Parser Agent (Multimodal Reader)

* ### **Role:** Processes all files uploaded by both builders (syllabi, instructions) and fillers (resumes, diagrams).

* ### **Responsibilities:**

  * ### Extracts text content and context from resumes or worksheets.

  * ### Analyzes visual artifacts (e.g., schemas, IDs) using multimodal vision processing to output semantic summaries for the Conversation Orchestrator.

* ### **Core Technology:** Gemini Multimodal (Vision-enabled) models.

### 5\. Session Lifecycle & Resource Agent (Billing & Rate Limiting)

* ### **Role:** Monitors the technical state of the websocket and audio connections to optimize billing.

* ### **Responsibilities:**

  * ### Tracks audio streaming inputs for silence or lack of user engagement.

  * ### Manages credit consumption rates and communicates with the credit ledger to suspend run-away tasks or inactive sessions.

###
